# UC-22.56.10 · TSA-SD Passenger-Rail 24-Hour Reporting (SD-1582-2022-01) — Amtrak + PTC + transit cyber events

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Same 24-hour clock as freight rail, but for passenger trains — and for transit operators that get federal money, we also tell the transit funding agency.*

---

## Description

Passenger-rail 24-hour reporting mirror with FTA parallel branch for federally-funded transit agencies.

## Value

Major transit operators (NJT, MTA, BART, WMATA, Caltrain) face dual TSA + FTA + FRA scrutiny on cyber events.

## Implementation

Same as UC-22.56.9 with passenger-rail register and FTA branch instead of (or in addition to) FRA branch.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.56.10` archived every 5 minutes to `audit_evidence` with `tsa_sd_clause="TSA-SD-1582-2022-01-s2"`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Trigger synthetic IR ticket with `tsa_passenger_rail_in_scope=true` and 23-hour-old awareness. UC-22.56.10 MUST escalate.

### Negative scenario

Submit within 24h with FTA notification time set. UC-22.56.10 MUST record `submission_status=on_time` and `fta_parallel=notified`.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

Same as UC-22.56.9.

## Step 1 — Passenger-rail register

```ini
[tsa_passenger_rail_operator_lookup]
field.passenger_rail_operator_id = string
field.operator_name = string
field.tsa_in_scope = string
field.fta_grant_recipient = string
field.ptc_vendor = string
```

## Step 2 — SOAR playbook

`tsa_passenger_rail_cisa_submission`: mirrors `tsa_cisa_submission` with FTA parallel-routing on grant-funded operators.

## Step 3 — Runbook

Same structure as UC-22.56.1. Add FTA branch when `fta_grant_recipient=true`.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

**Multi-jurisdictional operators** (e.g. NJT into NYC): coordinate with multiple state agencies; the CC documents the decision tree.

**TSA-specific failure modes**:
- **Federal funding revocation risk**: missing FTA notification on grant-funded agency can affect federal funding.
- **DHS / DOT inter-agency coordination**: CISA submission triggers DOT consultation; ensure the CC participates.

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sn_si_incident" tsa_passenger_rail_in_scope=true tsa_reportable_category=*
| eval awareness_time = coalesce(tsa_awareness_time, opened_at)
| eval cisa_submission_time = strptime(cisa_submission_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval hours_since_awareness = round((now() - awareness_time) / 3600, 2)
| eval hours_to_submission = case(
    isnotnull(cisa_submission_time), round((cisa_submission_time - awareness_time) / 3600, 2),
    1=1, hours_since_awareness)
| eval submission_status = case(
    isnotnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_to_submission <= 24, "on_time",
    isnotnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_to_submission > 24, "late",
    isnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_since_awareness > 23 AND hours_since_awareness <= 24, "clock_burning",
    isnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_since_awareness > 24, "missed",
    1=1, "in_window")
| eval fta_parallel = case(
    fta_grant_recipient=true AND isnotnull(fta_notification_time), "notified",
    fta_grant_recipient=true AND isnull(fta_notification_time), "missing",
    1=1, "na")
| where submission_status IN ("clock_burning","missed","late") OR fta_parallel="missing"
| table incident_id passenger_rail_operator_id tsa_reportable_category awareness_time cisa_submission_time hours_to_submission submission_status fta_parallel
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Live clock-burn. (Panel 2) FTA + FRA parallel-notification status. (Panel 3) Submission performance. (Panel 4) Late/missed table.

## Known False Positives

1. **Commuter rail/transit overlap**: many transit agencies operate both. Tag for both UC-22.56.10 and UC-22.56.17.
2. **PTC vs Train Control System**: PTC-related events may not be FTA-reportable but are CISA-reportable.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `tsa_22_56_10_exceptions`.

## References

- [TSA SD-1582-2022-01 (passenger rail cybersecurity)](https://www.tsa.gov/sites/default/files/sd-1582-2022-01.pdf)
- [TSA reissues passenger rail cyber requirements (Oct 2024)](https://www.tsa.gov/news/press/releases/2024/10/24/tsa-reissues-cybersecurity-requirements-rail-industry)
- [FTA Cybersecurity Information](https://www.transit.dot.gov/regulations-and-guidance/safety/cybersecurity)
